navigation.zone.agent_readiness.overview: See how ready your site is for AI agents
AI tools like ChatGPT and Gemini can't find or use your content yet. A few quick changes will get you started.
Your site is online, but AI tools may have trouble finding and understanding your content.
You've taken the first steps. A few more changes will make it much easier for AI tools to work with your site.

Your site works well with AI tools. They can find your content, verify who they are, and use your services.
Your site is fully set up for AI. Everything is in place for AI tools to find, read, and transact with your content.
All categories are looking good. Keep it up!

navigation.zone.agent_readiness.diagnost: See how ready your site is for AI agents, bots, and automated discovery.
navigation.zone.agent_readiness.ai_playg: The AI Playground is a no-code environment for testing Cloudflare's AI models right in your browser. Compare prompts and responses across models, and see how AI agents interpret your content before you ship changes.
navigation.zone.agent_readiness.aeo.desc: Optimize how AI answer engines discover, cite, and represent your content. This tab is coming soon.
Finish onboarding to use Agent Readiness
You must update your nameservers to activate your domain on Cloudflare before you can see how ready your site is for AI agents.
navigation.zone.agent_readiness.checks.r: Let Cloudflare publish and maintain a managed robots.txt, with AI-crawler rules and Content Signals for agents
navigation.zone.agent_readiness.checks.m: Automatically convert HTML to Markdown for agents that request it
You need zone settings edit permission to enable this
navigation.zone.agent_readiness.checks.l: Add link response headers so agents can discover useful resources such as your API documentation
navigation.zone.agent_readiness.checks.m: Set up Managed OAuth to allow agents to discover how to authenticate with your APIs
navigation.zone.agent_readiness.checks.p: Charge AI crawlers to access your content with Pay Per Crawl

navigation.zone.agent_readiness.redirect: Unable to scan — site is behind Cloudflare Access
This hostname redirected to a Cloudflare Access login page. The scan couldn't reach the actual site content. This also means AI agents won't be able to access this site without authenticating.
The requested domain redirected to <0>%{redirectedUrl}</0> during scanning. The results below are for the redirected site.
navigation.zone.agent_readiness.diagnost: Informational. Not counted towards your readiness score.
Copy the instructions below and paste them into your coding agent (Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, Copilot, etc.) to improve your site's agent readiness.

navigation.zone.apps.title: Apps
navigation.zone.rules.snippets.descripti:
Snippets provide a flexible and powerful way to customize the behavior of your website or application using short pieces of JavaScript code. Create snippets to customize HTTP response headers, implement JWT validation, define complex redirect functionality, and more.
Each snippet has an associated rule that defines when the snippet will run.
navigation.zone.rules.snippets.page.desc: Short, powerful pieces of JavaScript that dynamically tailor your website or application.

navigation.zone.agent_readiness.performa: % of AI answers
share of cited content
AI judge · 1–5
Share of the AI answers we sampled that cited your site. Scale 0–100% — higher is better.
Of the content AI answers cite, how much is yours — weighted toward earlier sentences. Averaged across all sampled answers, so answers that don't cite you count as 0%. Scale 0–100%.

An AI judge's rating (1–5) of how strong your citation is, averaged over 7 dimensions: relevance, influence, unique information, distinct perspective, authority, follow-up likelihood, and click-through likelihood. It measures citation strength, not sentiment.
More information
Under 20%
50% and up
What the ring means
Based on %{runs} sampled answers · %{samples} per assistant · scanned %{when}
AI answers are sampled and vary run to run, so treat these as estimates.
Beta
An experimental signal rated by an AI model — directional, not definitive.
What this means for you
How your site shows up in AI answers — and where to focus next.
AI assistants cite your site in %{visibility} of answers — you’re a go-to source for these questions.
AI assistants cite your site in %{visibility} of answers — you’re gaining traction but not yet a default source.
AI assistants cite your site in only %{visibility} of answers — you’re rarely part of the conversation yet.
%{cited} cites your site, but %{missing} doesn’t yet.
Coverage varies by assistant — strengthen the content and signals %{missing} relies on.
How AI assistants see your site
The two signals that matter most for AEO — how often you’re cited, and how central you are when you are.
How on-topic your citation is to the question asked.
How much your citation shapes the assistant’s answer.
Whether you offer information other cited sources don’t.
Whether you add a distinct perspective to the answer.
How authoritative your site appears as a source.
How likely a reader is to dig deeper into your site.
How likely a reader is to click through to your site.
AI assistants don’t cite you for %{prompt} — %{competitors} own that answer.
Publish a focused page that answers it directly, then study %{top}’s page and cover what it leaves out.
You’re cited for %{prompt} — but so are %{competitors}.
To become the primary source, lead the page with a concise, quotable answer and add FAQ or Product structured data so assistants can lift it verbatim.
You’re cited for %{prompt}, but you’re only %{prominence} of the answer.
Move your key facts to the top of the page, clearly stated and attributed, so more of the answer comes from you.
Then make sure these pages are crawlable by AI assistants — check robots.txt and structured data in Diagnostics.
